blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Pulmonary-Cardiovascular Interaction?
(1) Oxygen delivery = ventilation × cardiac output: ทั้ง oxygenation และ blood flow จำเป็นสำหรับ tissue oxygen delivery — แก้แค่ปอดโดยไม่สนใจ hemodynamics คือ incomplete care. (2) MAP ที่สูงเกินไปทำลายหัวใจ: high MAP → ↓ venous return → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LV preload → ↓ cardiac output — ventilator setting เดียวกันที่ช่วยปอดอาจทำร้ายหัวใจ. (3) MAP ที่ต่ำเกินไปก็ทำลายปอด: underinflation → ↑ PVR (U-shaped curve) → ↑ RV afterload → pulmonary hypertension. (4) Cord clamping timing = first cardiorespiratory interaction: clamp ก่อน lung aeration → ↓ LV preload acutely → ↓ cardiac output. ต้องเข้าใจ physiology based cord clamping. (5) CO2 เชื่อมกับ brain injury: hypercapnia >52 mmHg → ↑ CBF reactivity → ↑ IVH risk; hypocapnia → ↓ CBF → PVL. Target CO2 critical. (6) BPD-PH เป็น hidden complication: pulmonary hypertension in BPD พบ 8-42% ใน ELBW — ถ้าไม่ screen จะ miss และ mortality ↑↑.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย fetal circulation และ cardiorespiratory events ของ perinatal transition — รวม physiologically based cord clamping
อธิบาย effects ของ positive-pressure ventilation ต่อ preload, contractility, afterload ของ RV และ LV แยกกัน
เข้าใจ U-shaped PVR-lung volume curve: ทั้ง underinflation และ overinflation ↑ PVR เท่ากัน
วิเคราะห์ effects ของ PEEP/MAP/CPAP ต่อ cardiac output ใน preterm — เปรียบเทียบ animal vs human data
อธิบาย effect ของ permissive hypercapnia และ acidosis ต่อ myocardial contractility, CBF, และ IVH risk ใน preterm
เชื่อม PPHN pathophysiology กับ vicious cycle ของ ↑ PVR → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LVO → ↓ systemic BP → ↑ R-to-L shunt
อธิบาย cardiorespiratory implications ของ PDA ต่อ lung compliance, BPD, และ pulmonary hemorrhage
ระบุ hemodynamic effects ของ surfactant administration: RVO, SVC flow, CBF changes
ระบุ CO2 threshold ที่เพิ่ม IVH risk: breakpoint ที่ ~52 mmHg ใน preterm
วางแผน screen และ manage pulmonary hypertension ใน BPD: echo indices, prevalence data, risk factors
1. Cardiorespiratory Interactions at Birth
การ transition จากชีวิตใน utero มาสู่การหายใจเองเป็น cardiorespiratory interaction ครั้งแรกและสำคัญที่สุด — ความล้มเหลวใน system ใด system หนึ่งสามารถ cascade ไปทำลาย system อีกฝ่ายได้อย่างรวดเร็ว.
1.1 Fetal Circulation — สิ่งที่ต่างจาก postnatal

In utero: ปอดได้รับ pulmonary blood flow เพียง ~10% of postnatal flow (Rasanen J et al. Circulation 1996;94:1068). สาเหตุ: high PVR จาก hypoxia + vasoconstricting factors → RV ejects mostly via PDA right-to-left into systemic circulation
Fetal shunts: Ductus venosus → IVC → foramen ovale → LA: oxygenated placental blood bypasses ปอดไปสู่ systemic circulation. PDA: R-to-L shunt — descending aorta → umbilical arteries → placenta.

Main LA preload source in utero = placental return via IVC/FO, NOT pulmonary veins (PBF ต่ำมาก). เมื่อ umbilical cord clamped ก่อน lung aeration → acute loss of LA filling → ↓ LV preload → ↓ cardiac output (Bhatt S et al. Front Pediatr 2014;2:113)
1.2 Key Events of Normal Transition
Step 1 — Lung inflation (crying or PPV): ↑ PBF rapidly. PDA reverses L-to-R (Crossley KJ et al. J Physiol 2009;587:4695). Lung liquid absorbed via transepithelial gradients during inspiration.

Step 2 — ↑ PBF fills LA: adequate LA filling established → safe to cut umbilical cord. ลำดับที่ถูกต้อง: inflate lungs → establish PBF → clamp cord = ทารกได้ placental transfusion + ปอดทำงานก่อนที่ placenta จะถูกตัดออก.

ถ้าเรียงลำดับผิด (clamp cord ก่อน lung aeration): ↑ arterial pressure → ↑ blood flow surge → then ↓ cardiac output rapidly as PBF not yet established. Hemodynamic instability รุนแรงกว่า physiologically based cord clamping (Hooper SB et al. Matern Health Neonatol Perinatol 2016;2:4)

Physiologically Based Cord Clamping (PBCC) — หลักฐานและ practice
แนวคิด: clamp cord หลังจาก lung aeration confirmed — ↓ hemodynamic instability เพราะ PBF established ก่อน placental flow ถูกตัด. Delayed cord clamping (DCC): ≥30-60 sec → ↑ likelihood ทารก establishes breathing before clamp. Benefits previously ascribed to placental transfusion เท่านั้น แต่ hemodynamic stability ก็สำคัญ. Umbilical cord milking (UCM): alternative; faster transfusion, less respiratory effect on volume. ↑ mean BP, ↓ inotrope use (Katheria A et al. PLoS One 2014;9:e94085). AVOID: excessively high CPAP at delivery room — high initial CPAP/PPV can also ↓ venous return and compromise cardiovascular function.
2. Effects of Positive-Pressure Ventilation on the Cardiovascular System
2.1 Overview — Preload, Contractility, Afterload

Mechanical ventilation ส่งผลต่อ 3 components ของ cardiac function: preload (via intrathoracic pressure & PBF), contractility (indirect via PVR & acidosis), afterload (PVR for RV; intrathoracic pressure for LV). ผลต่อ RV และ LV ตรงข้ามกัน ในหลายด้าน.
2.2 RV vs LV — ทำไมผลต่างกัน
RV: รับ venous return จาก outside thorax → PPV ↑ intrathoracic pressure → impedes venous return → ↓ RV filling + ↑ RV afterload (PVR) → ↓ RVO. RV เป็น primary victim ของ high MAP

LV: รับ venous return จาก inside thorax (pulmonary veins) → PPV ↑ intra-alveolar pressure → squeezes blood from pulmonary capillaries → ↑ LV filling. ↑ intrathoracic pressure → ↓ transmyocardial pressure gradient → ↓ LV afterload → potentially ↑ LV function (Cheifetz IM. Respir Care 2014;59:1937)

ดังนั้น: ↑ MAP → RV bad (↓ CO), LV may benefit short-term แต่ถ้า ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LA filling → eventually LV output ↓ ด้วย. Ventricular interdependence: RV dilation + ↑ pressure → septal bowing → ↓ LV cavity + ↓ LV preload
2.3 PVR-Lung Volume Relationship (U-Shaped Curve)

KEY CONCEPT: ทั้ง overinflation และ underinflation เพิ่ม PVR เท่ากัน — optimal PVR อยู่ที่ FRC. กราฟ U-shaped: (1) ↓ lung volume (atelectasis): alveolar vessels collapse → ↑ resistance. (2) ↑ lung volume (overdistension): alveolar vessels compressed by distended alveoli → ↑ alveolar resistance. Extraalveolar vessels behavior ตรงข้าม: ↑ volume → less tortuous → ↓ resistance.

Clinical implication: ทั้ง under-PEEP (atelectasis) และ over-PEEP (overdistension) → ↑ PVR → ↑ RV afterload → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF. การ optimize lung volume ที่ FRC จึงสำคัญที่สุดใน management ของ ventilated newborn
3. Effects of PEEP, CPAP, and MAP — Clinical Data
3.1 Animal Studies

Polglase et al. J Appl Physiol 2005;99:1453: premature lambs (normal lungs), baseline PEEP 4 cmH₂O: ↑ PEEP to 8 → ↓ PBF 20.5%; ↑ to 12 → ↓ PBF 41%. Paradox: oxygenation improved (↑ recruitment + V/Q matching) despite ↓ PBF.

Caveat: animal studies use NORMAL lung compliance → PEEP above 4 cmH₂O = excess pressure → large hemodynamic effects. Human neonates have lung disease = ↓ compliance → less pressure transmitted to pleura → smaller hemodynamic effects.
3.2 Human Neonatal Studies

Hausdorf & Hellwege Crit Care Med 1987;15:661: PEEP 0→8 cmH₂O ใน preterm post-acute RDS: ↓ ventricular stroke volume and cardiac output 25-30%. No effect on blood pressure (BP maintained via compensatory ↑ SVR — inotropy preserved at this age).
Trang et al. Crit Care Med 1988;16:1094: cardiac output compromise begins at PEEP >6 cmH₂O in preterm. แต่ขึ้นกับ lung compliance.

de Waal et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2007;92:F444: PEEP 5→8 cmH₂O ใน ventilated preterm+term: nonsignificant ↓ RVO 5%; SVC flow unchanged overall. Variable: 1/3 ↑ SVC flow, 1/3 ↓ SVC flow → depends on underlying lung compliance. Compliant lung → ↓ flow with ↑ PEEP; atelectatic lung → ↑ flow with ↑ PEEP.

de Waal et al. J Pediatr 2009;154:651: HFOV lung recruitment (MAP up to 20 cmH₂O!) ใน preterm with RDS: ↓ RVO 17% แต่ SVC flow unchanged, no change in ductal diameter/flow. สรุป: เมื่อ lung compliance ต่ำ (lung disease), แม้ MAP สูงมากก็มีผลน้อยต่อ hemodynamics

CPAP 4/6/8 cmH₂O (Beker et al. J Pediatr 2014): stable preterm with minimal lung disease: NO hemodynamic difference across CPAP levels — attributed to ineffective pressure transmission via nasal interface.

Rule: Lung Compliance กำหนดว่า MAP จะส่งผลต่อ Hemodynamics มากแค่ไหน
สูตรคิด: ยิ่ง lung compliant มาก → pressure transmission to pleura มากขึ้น → hemodynamic effect มากขึ้น. RDS (low compliance) → MAP absorbed โดยปอดก่อน → น้อยไปถึงหัวใจ. BPD recovering (↑ compliance): เมื่อ lung compliance ดีขึ้น — pressure ส่งไปถึง pleura มากขึ้น → same PEEP ที่ previous OK อาจ compromise hemodynamics. ดังนั้น wean PEEP/MAP ตามที่ compliance ดีขึ้น ไม่ใช่รอ oxygenation ต่ำลงก่อน.
4. Permissive Hypercapnia, Acidosis, and Cardiovascular Effects
4.1 Acid-Base Effects on Myocardium
Acidosis → pH-sensitive ionic channels in myocyte: net ↑ intracellular Ca²⁺ (promotes contraction), แต่ myofibrillar responsiveness ↓ in acidic pH → net ↓ contractility despite ↑ Ca²⁺.
Adults: acidosis → ↓ myocardial contractility + ↓ SVR → cardiac output may paradoxically ↑ (low afterload). Hypercapnia effects mediated mainly via pH change → resolve with metabolic compensation.

Neonates differ: newborn myocardium more tolerant + more vasodilation (Nakanishi T et al. Circ Res 1990;67:111). Noori S et al. J Pediatr 2013;162:958: pH 7.02-7.46 in hemodynamically stable preterm: no relationship between pH and contractility in first 2 weeks. Post-transitional (Day 4-14): weak ↑ LVO with ↑ CO2; ↓ SVR with ↑ CO2 — pattern approaching adults.

Sodium bicarbonate correction: ↑ pH 7.24 → 7.30 in ventilated preterm: transient ↑ cardiac output — likely volume effect only, not sustained hemodynamic benefit (Fanconi et al. Intensive Care Med 1993;19:65). NaHCO3 also no effect on cerebral/renal/splanchnic rSO2.
4.2 CO2, Cerebral Blood Flow, and IVH Risk

CO2 เป็น potent cerebrovascular vasodilator: hypercapnia → ↑ CBF; hypocapnia → ↓ CBF. Effect blunted on day 1, แต่ by day 2 cerebral vasculature becomes very reactive to CO2 (Pryds O et al. J Pediatr 1989;115:638)

Noori et al. Acta Paediatr 2014;103:e334: breakpoint CO2 vs MCA flow velocity: no relationship below ~52 mmHg; strong linear relationship above. Implication: เหนือ 52 mmHg → ↑ CBF steeply + ↓ autoregulation

Kaiser et al. J Perinatol 2006;26:279: maximum CO2 ใน 3 วันแรก = dose-dependent predictor of severe P/IVH. Fabres et al. Pediatrics 2007;119:299: both extremes of CO2 AND magnitude of CO2 fluctuations → severe IVH.

Mechanism CO2 → IVH: hypercapnia → ↑ CBF → ↑ cerebrovascular pressure → ↓ autoregulation → potentiates reperfusion injury after ischemic episode (Noori S, Seri I. Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2015;20:232)

Hypocapnia → PVL: ↓ CBF → ischemia → PVL. Wiswell et al. Pediatrics 1996;98:918: hypocarbia during HFJV → ↑ cystic PVL. Shankaran et al. Pediatrics 2006;118:1654: cumulative hypocarbia index = independent risk factor for PVL.

CO2 Target ใน Preterm NICU: หาจุดสมดุล
เป้าหมาย: pCO2 45–55 mmHg (permissive hypercapnia) — ต่ำพอที่จะลด lung injury (ใช้ lower settings) แต่ไม่สูงเกินจนทำลาย CBF autoregulation. Critical: ≤52 mmHg ใน 3 วันแรก ลด IVH risk จาก CBF reactivity breakpoint. Avoid pCO2 <35 mmHg at all times: hypocapnia → PVL + auto-triggering (ดู Ch.16 lesson). PHELBI trial (Thome UH, Lancet Respir Med 2015;3:534): high vs mild permissive hypercapnia ใน ELBW: BPD rates similar. Neurodevelopmental outcomes similar (PHELBI follow-up Arch Dis Child FN 2017). ดังนั้น aggressive permissive hypercapnia ไม่ได้ดีกว่า moderate และ เสี่ยง IVH มากกว่า.
5. Effect of the Cardiovascular System on the Respiratory System
5.1 Mechanisms
Myocardial dysfunction (asphyxia, sepsis): ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → hypoxemic respiratory failure + ↑ PVR (vicious cycle). Concomitant PPHN → even more ↓ PBF.

Excessive PBF (PDA, intracardiac shunt): fluid leakage into interstitium → ↓ lung compliance → ↑ respiratory support needed. Pulmonary hemorrhage = hemorrhagic pulmonary edema: acute ↑ PBF (large PDA L-to-R shunt) → pulmonary capillary pressure ↑↑ → hemorrhage (Kluckow M, Evans N. J Pediatr 2000;137:68)

Impaired LV diastolic function: ↑ LA pressure → pulmonary venous congestion → pulmonary edema → ↑ respiratory support. Often unrecognized because noninvasive assessment of diastolic function is difficult — look for pulmonary venous congestion pattern on CXR + echo LA size.
5.2 PDA and the Lung

PDA hemodynamic stages: (1) Initial hours: PDA R-to-L or bidirectional (high PVR). (2) As lung compliance improves (surfactant, natural RDS course): PVR ↓ → PDA shifts L-to-R → ↑ PBF → stiff lungs → ↑ CO2 retention/apnea. (3) Large L-to-R PDA + ↑ PBF → ↑ capillary pressure → interstitial edema → ↓ compliance → ↑ ventilator support → ↑ BPD risk

Surfactant + lung compliance improvement → ↑ L-to-R PDA shunting (animal studies: Clyman RI et al. J Pediatr 1982;100:101). Human studies have not consistently shown this increase in ductal shunting post-surfactant, possibly due to Doppler technique limitations.

PDA–BPD association: clear association but causal relationship not established. Clinical trials of early PDA treatment have not shown ↓ BPD — confounded by open-label treatment and patient heterogeneity (Zonnenberg I, de Waal K. Acta Paediatr 2012;101:247)
6. Clinical Conditions with Cardiorespiratory Interaction
6.1 PPHN — Vicious Cycle

PPHN คือ clinical syndrome, not a single entity — hypoxic respiratory failure disproportionate to lung abnormality. Components: lung pathology (V/Q mismatch), myocardial dysfunction, PDA shunt direction, PVR, SVR. เข้าใจ vicious cycle เป็นสิ่งสำคัญที่สุดใน management.
Vicious cycle: ↑ PVR → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LA filling → ↓ LVO → ↓ systemic BP → ↑ R-to-L PDA shunt → ↑ desaturation → ↑ hypoxia/acidosis → ↑ PVR (back to start)

Asphyxia contribution: up to 70% of asphyxiated infants have abnormal cardiac function (Sehgal A et al. Cardiovasc Ultrasound 2013;11:34). ↓ LVO exacerbated by myocardial injury → ↓ systemic BP → ↑ R-to-L ductal shunt.
Ventricular interdependence: ↑ PVR → RV dilation + ↑ pressure → septal bowing into LV cavity → ↓ LV preload/compliance → ↓ LVO (McNamara P et al. In: Avery's Neonatology 7th ed, 2016, pp 457-486)
IAtrogenic component: high MAP mechanical ventilation → ↑ PVR from overdistension → compresses pulmonary vasculature → ↑ vicious cycle. Balance oxygenation vs hemodynamics เสมอ.
6.2 CDH — Multiple Interacting Factors

CDH = lung hypoplasia + pulmonary vasculature abnormality → ↑ PVR → PPHN. Additional: heart displaced by herniated organs, LV hypoplasia (variable degree) → limits pulmonary vasodilator therapy response (Kinsella JP et al. Semin Perinatol 2005;29:123)

Systemic hypotension in CDH: multifactorial — ↓ venous return + hypoxia + acidosis + LV dysfunction secondary to abnormal septal motion. LV hypoplasia common in left-sided CDH: ↑ severity = smaller fetal LV size (Byrne FA et al. Ultrasound Obstet Gynecol 2015)
6.3 Surfactant Hemodynamic Effects
Surfactant administration → hemodynamic changes mostly from mechanical effects of instillation + rapid improvement in compliance + FRC, not direct drug effect.

Sehgal A et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2010;95:F345: delivery room surfactant ใน VLBW: ↑ RVO แต่ ↓ LVO (despite ↑ ductal diameter + ↑ L-to-R shunt) — LV output paradoxically ↓. Intriguing: ↑ fractional shortening + ↑ LA/Ao ratio but ↓ LVO — suggests ductal shunt diverted pulmonary flow.
Katheria & Leone J Perinatol 2013;33:525: rescue surfactant ใน mature preterm: ↑ SVC flow + ↑ RVO แต่ only in surfactant-responders (FiO2 0.21 by 3h). Non-responders: no hemodynamic change.
CBF effects variable: some studies ↓ CBF (redistribution + ↑ CO2), others ↑ or no change — instillation technique matters: rapid bolus → larger CBF swings.
7. Pulmonary Hypertension in BPD — Post-Transitional Concern
7.1 Epidemiology and Pathophysiology

BPD-PH pathophysiology: lung injury + inflammation + arrest of pulmonary vascular development → ↓ vascular density → ↑ PVR. PH กพบได้แม้ใน mild/no BPD (Mourani PM et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2015;191:87)
Prevalence ranges:
~8% ELBW at 4 weeks (retrospective, quant + qual echo indices) (Collaco JM et al. PLoS One 2016;11:e0163904)
~12-18% ELBW after first month of life (prospective, TR jet + qualitative) (Bhat R et al. Pediatrics 2012;129:e682)
~15% ELBW with BPD at 4 weeks (Slaughter JL et al. J Perinatol 2011;31:635)
42% incidence at 7 days (birth weight 500-1250g) — early PH = risk factor for BPD severity and PH at 36 wk PMA (Mourani PM 2015). Suggests pulmonary vascular disease independent of BPD.
7.2 Echo Diagnosis

Primary: TR jet velocity → estimated RVSP. Limitation: TR jet absent in up to 25% of PH cases (Abman SH et al. Circulation 2015;132:2037 — AHA/ATS guidelines). Echo may not accurately estimate severity even when TR present.

Secondary qualitative indices: interventricular septal flattening, RV dilation, RV hypertrophy. Less reliable but important when TR absent. Pulmonary venous velocity also useful: ↓ velocity = ↑ PVR (Jain A, McNamara PJ. Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2015;20:262)
PDA shunting: often not useful for estimating PH in BPD — PDA usually closed in chronic BPD patients.
7.3 Risk Factors for BPD-PH

rows: Failure to treat underlying lung disease

blocks.text: Outcomes: BPD-PH ↑ mortality + poor neurodevelopmental outcome (Nakanishi H et al. J Perinatol 2016;36:890). Screen high-risk preterm ที่ 36 weeks PMA ด้วย echo — AHA/ATS guidelines 2015.
8. NICU Practical — Applying Cardiorespiratory Physiology at Bedside

Step 1 — Optimize lung volume (FRC): aim for lung volume at FRC — PVR nadir. Use optimal PEEP: 5-6 cmH₂O standard; recruit if FiO2 high + atelectasis on CXR; wean PEEP as compliance recovers (↑ compliance → more pressure to pleura → hemodynamic risk).

Step 2 — Monitor pCO2 carefully: target 45-55 mmHg (preterm), avoid >52 in first 3 days (IVH breakpoint), avoid <35 (PVL + auto-trigger). TcCO2 monitoring or frequent BGAs in first 72h of life ใน ELBW.

Step 3 — Assess hemodynamics in sick ventilated newborn: echo-guided: RVO (normal 150-300 mL/kg/min), SVC flow (normal 41-98 mL/kg/min), LVO, ductal shunt direction + velocity, TR jet. SVC flow = best non-ductal systemic flow marker in early transition (PDA confounds LVO).

Step 4 — Suspect BPD-PH early: ใน infant ที่ FiO2 requirement ↑ ไม่สัมพันธ์กับ lung disease severity, cyanotic episodes, desaturation → echo for TR jet, septal flattening, RV size. Screen ที่ 36 wk PMA ใน high-risk BPD.

Step 5 — PPHN management: optimize gas exchange + cardiac output simultaneously: (1) MAP ต้องพอสำหรับ lung recruitment (FiO2 ↓) แต่ไม่ compress pulmonary vasculature (↓ PBF). (2) iNO 20 ppm: selective pulmonary vasodilator — ↓ PVR without ↓ SVR. (3) Avoid hyperoxia: ↓ response to iNO (Lakshminrusimha S et al. Pediatr Res 2009;66:539). (4) Dobutamine/milrinone สำหรับ RV dysfunction. (5) Correct acidosis (metabolic component).

Step 6 — Cord clamping in delivery room: delay ≥30-60 sec or until breathing established. If infant vigorous → allow lung aeration first. PBCC approach: PPV while cord intact เมื่อ resuscitation required — ลด hemodynamic instability.

Step 7 — Post-surfactant monitoring: after surfactant: ↓ MAP/PEEP promptly as compliance improves — compliance↑ → same PEEP now relatively high → hemodynamic compromise risk. Check for ↑ L-to-R PDA shunt → pulmonary edema → ↑ FiO2.
9. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับใช้พรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — MAP ที่ดีที่สุดคือ MAP ที่ให้ FRC: ไม่ใช่ MAX oxygenation
The optimal balance (Fig. 17.4): PPV ↑ alveolar oxygenation แต่ ↑ MAP ↓ PBF. Oxygen delivery = oxygenation × cardiac output × Hgb. ↑ FiO2 ถ้า SpO2 ต่ำ ก่อนที่จะ ↑ MAP — เพิ่ม MAP เมื่อ lung volume ต่ำชัดเจน (atelectasis on CXR, worsening compliance). อย่า ↑ MAP เพราะ oxygenation ไม่ดีโดยไม่ประเมิน hemodynamics ก่อน: อาจ ↑ FiO2 ↓ PBF ทำให้ tissue oxygen delivery ไม่ดีขึ้น.

PEARL 2 — Compliance ↑ = Time to Wean PEEP (ก่อนที่ Hemodynamics จะ Suffer)
เมื่อ lung compliance ดีขึ้น (surfactant response, recovery from RDS): same PEEP ที่เคย appropriate ตอน lung stiff ตอนนี้ถูก transmitted มาก→ pleural pressure ↑ → RV preload ↓ → ↓ cardiac output. Signs: BP drop หลัง surfactant, SpO2 ปกติ แต่ CRT prolonged → echo ↓ SVC flow. Action: ↓ PEEP 1 cmH₂O + ↓ PIP proportionally (VTV จะปรับเอง). Wean MAP/PEEP actively after surfactant — อย่ารอ until oxygenation deteriorates.

PEARL 3 — pCO2 >52 mmHg ใน 3 วันแรก = IVH Breakpoint
Noori et al. 2014: CO2 threshold ~52 mmHg — above this, strong linear ↑ CBF + ↓ CBF autoregulation. ↓ autoregulation = brain vulnerable to BP swings → reperfusion injury → IVH. Clinical rule: ถ้า pCO2 ↑ จาก 48 → 55 mmHg ใน ELBW day 2-3 → แก้ด่วน. Check: auto-trigger? RR too low? VT too small? ↑ ventilation settings หรือ ↑ rate temporarily. Permissive hypercapnia หมายถึง 45-55 ไม่ใช่ 55-70 ใน 3 วันแรก.

PEARL 4 — PPHN Vicious Cycle: Break ที่ PVR + Cardiac Output พร้อมกัน
PPHN ไม่ใช่แค่ lung problem — เป็น cardiopulmonary circuit problem. ↑ PVR → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LVO → ↓ BP → ↑ R-to-L shunt → ↑ hypoxia → ↑ PVR. Break the cycle: (1) iNO 20 ppm — ↓ PVR selectively. (2) Optimize lung volume (FRC) — ↓ PVR at FRC nadir. (3) Dobutamine/milrinone — support RV function. (4) Avoid hyperoxia — ↓ iNO efficacy. (5) Target pH 7.35-7.45 — correct metabolic acidosis. อย่าทำ MAP สูงเพื่อ oxygenate: overdistension ↑ PVR ยิ่งทำให้แย่.

PEARL 5 — Screen BPD-PH ที่ 36 wk PMA: อย่า Miss
BPD-PH พบ 8-42% ใน ELBW — often clinically silent until late decompensation. Red flags: FiO2 requirement เพิ่มขึ้น unexpectedly, cyanotic spells, poor growth, prolonged oxygen need. Screen: echo ที่ 36 wk PMA ใน high-risk BPD (ELBW, prolonged vent, SGA, oligohydramnios, PDA ligation). Look for: TR jet velocity, septal flattening, RV dilation/hypertrophy, pulmonary venous velocity. Early detection → sildenafil/bosentan → ↓ mortality, ↑ neurodevelopmental outcome.
10. Evidence Update — Key Citations
Noori S & Kluckow M. The Newborn Lung Ch.17, 2019: Comprehensive framework for pulmonary-cardiovascular interaction — primary reference this lesson.

Cheifetz IM. Respir Care 2014;59:1937: Classic review — intrathoracic pressure effects on LV afterload. Fig 17.1 source: LV needs less transmural pressure during PPV (transmyocardial gradient ↓ with ↑ intrathoracic P).

Hausdorf G, Hellwege HH. Crit Care Med 1987;15:661: PEEP 0→8 cmH₂O in preterm post-RDS: 25-30% ↓ stroke volume + cardiac output. First study demonstrating significant hemodynamic impact in preterm neonates.

de Waal KA et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2007;92:F444: PEEP 5→8 cmH₂O in ventilated neonates: variable SVC flow — 1/3 ↑, 1/3 ↓ — explained by lung compliance differences. Compliant lung → ↓ flow, atelectatic → ↑ flow.
de Waal K et al. J Pediatr 2009;154:651: HFOV lung recruitment (MAP 20 cmH₂O) in preterm RDS: only ↓ RVO 17%, SVC flow unchanged — confirms low compliance lung buffers hemodynamic effect of high MAP.

Noori S, Anderson M et al. Acta Paediatr 2014;103:e334: CO2 breakpoint ~52 mmHg for CBF reactivity in preterm transition — strong linear relationship above threshold; no relationship below. Establishes safe upper CO2 limit.
Kaiser JR et al. J Perinatol 2006;26:279: Maximum CO2 in first 3 days = dose-dependent predictor of severe P/IVH in VLBW. Establishes early hypercapnia as modifiable IVH risk factor.
Fabres J et al. Pediatrics 2007;119:299: Both extremes AND magnitude of CO2 fluctuations → severe IVH in preterm. Highlights importance of CO2 stability, not just mean value.

Thome UH et al. Lancet Respir Med 2015;3:534 (PHELBI): High vs mild permissive hypercapnia in ELBW: no difference in BPD. Follow-up 2017: no difference in neurodevelopment. Aggressive hypercapnia not beneficial.
Mourani PM et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2015;191:87: 500-1250g preterm: 42% PH at day 7; early PH = risk factor for BPD severity + PH at 36 wk PMA. Pulmonary vascular disease independent of BPD.
Bhat R et al. Pediatrics 2012;129:e682: Prospective ELBW cohort: 12% PH after first month (TR jet), 18% with qualitative indices. Baseline epidemiology for BPD-PH screening programs.
Noori S, Seri I. Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2015;20:232: Hemodynamic antecedents of P/IVH in very preterm — CO2 → ↑ CBF + ↓ autoregulation → reperfusion injury pathway. Framework for IVH prevention.
Polglase GR et al. J Appl Physiol 2005;99:1453: Premature lambs, PEEP 4→8→12 cmH₂O: PBF ↓ 20.5% and 41%. Despite ↓ PBF, oxygenation improved (↑ recruitment). PEEP effects lung-compliance dependent.
Bhatt S et al. Front Pediatr 2014;2:113: Ventilation before cord clamping improves cardiovascular transition — physiologic basis for PBCC. Earlier LA filling → less hemodynamic instability at birth.
Abman SH et al. Circulation 2015;132:2037 (AHA/ATS PH Guidelines): TR jet absent in 25% of PH cases; echo may underestimate severity. Recommend screening high-risk BPD infants at 36 wk PMA.
11. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: อธิบาย U-shaped relationship ระหว่าง lung volume และ PVR — ทั้ง overinflation และ underinflation ↑ PVR ได้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: FRC = PVR nadir (optimal). (1) Underinflation (↓ lung volume): alveolar vessels collapse + supporting tissues collapse → ↑ alveolar vascular resistance. Extraalveolar vessels tortuous → ↑ resistance. (2) Overinflation (↑ lung volume): distended alveoli physically compress alveolar vessels → ↑ alveolar resistance. Clinical: เป้าหมายคือ maintain FRC — ไม่ให้ atelectasis (↑ PVR) ไม่ให้ overdistension (↑ PVR). ทั้งสองทาง → ↑ RV afterload → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LVO → ↓ cardiac output.
Q: ทำไม RV จึงได้รับผลกระทบจาก PPV มากกว่า LV? และ LV มี potential benefit อะไรจาก ↑ intrathoracic pressure?

ตอบ: RV: รับ venous return จาก outside thorax → PPV ↑ pleural pressure → ↓ transmural RA pressure → impeded venous return → ↓ RV preload. ↑ MAP → overdistension → ↑ PVR → ↑ RV afterload. Double hit: ↓ preload + ↑ afterload = ↓ RVO. LV benefit: receives venous return from inside thorax (pulmonary veins). ↑ intrathoracic pressure → ↑ intra-alveolar pressure → squeezes blood toward LA. Additionally ↓ transmyocardial pressure gradient = ↓ LV afterload (wall stress): LV needs less transmural pressure to achieve same systemic BP → potentially ↑ LV function. แต่ benefit นี้ depend on adequate RVO ก่อน.
Q: ทารก ELBW 26 wk, วันที่ 2 ของชีวิต, ventilated ด้วย A/C+VG, blood gas: pH 7.19, pCO2 58 mmHg, ควรจัดการอย่างไรเกี่ยวกับ CO2 target และทำไม?

ตอบ: pCO2 58 mmHg ใน ELBW วันที่ 2 = ABOVE the IVH breakpoint (~52 mmHg). Action: ↑ ventilation to bring pCO2 to 45-52 mmHg — options: (1) ↑ rate (A/C → ↑ backup rate or ↑ SIMV rate). (2) ↑ VT target slightly if plateau pressure allows. Why urgent: Day 2 = peak CBF reactivity to CO2. pCO2 >52 → strong ↑ CBF + ↓ autoregulation → brain vulnerable to BP swings → reperfusion injury → P/IVH (Kaiser 2006; Noori 2014). Avoid overcorrection: pCO2 <35 → hypocapnia → ↓ CBF → PVL + auto-trigger. Target: 45-52 mmHg ใน 3 วันแรก.
Q: อธิบาย vicious cycle ของ PPHN — และ iNO ช่วย break cycle ที่จุดไหน?

ตอบ: Vicious cycle: ↑ PVR → ↓ RVO → ↓ PBF → ↓ LA filling → ↓ LVO → ↓ systemic BP → ↑ R-to-L PDA/PFO shunt → ↑ desaturation → ↑ hypoxia + acidosis → ↑ PVR. iNO breaks at PVR: inhaled NO → guanylyl cyclase → ↑ cGMP → smooth muscle relaxation → selective pulmonary vasodilation (↓ PVR) without ↓ SVR — ↑ PBF → ↑ LA filling → ↑ LVO → ↑ systemic BP → ↓ R-to-L shunt → ↑ oxygenation → ↓ hypoxia → ↓ PVR. Dose: 20 ppm. Avoid hyperoxia: ↓ iNO response (Lakshminrusimha 2009). Optimize lung volume (FRC) ก่อน/ระหว่าง iNO — overdistension ↑ PVR และลดประสิทธิภาพ iNO.
Q: ทำไม PEEP ที่สูงถึง 20 cmH₂O ใน HFOV lung recruitment study ถึงมี hemodynamic effect น้อย?

ตอบ: de Waal 2009 study: MAP 20 cmH₂O → ↓ RVO เพียง 17%, SVC flow ไม่เปลี่ยน. เหตุผล: ทารกมี RDS = low lung compliance → ปอดที่ stiff absorbs airway pressure ก่อน → pressure ส่งไปถึง pleura น้อยมาก → hemodynamic effect ↓. Contrast: ถ้า lung compliance ปกติ (เช่น animal models) → PEEP 8 cmH₂O → ↓ PBF 20%. Clinical implication: lung disease protects hemodynamics from high MAP; แต่ WHEN compliance improves (surfactant, recovery) → same PEEP now transmits more pressure → hemodynamic compromise risk ↑. Wean PEEP actively as compliance improves.
Q: Surfactant administration ใน VLBW ทำให้ cardiac output เปลี่ยนอย่างไร? ทำไม LVO อาจลดลงแม้ RVO เพิ่ม?

ตอบ: Sehgal 2010 (delivery room surfactant): ↑ RVO แต่ ↓ LVO despite ↑ ductal diameter + ↑ fractional shortening + ↑ LA/Ao ratio. Paradox: ↑ ductal L-to-R shunting after surfactant diverts blood from systemic to pulmonary circulation → LV preload effectively ↓ (blood shunted back to pulmonary system before reaching systemic). Mechanism: surfactant → ↓ PVR → more blood flows L-to-R via PDA → pulmonary overcirculation → LVO ↓. This is why post-surfactant monitoring includes: watch for ↑ L-to-R PDA shunt, pulmonary edema, ↑ FiO2, and need for PEEP ↑.
Q: ระบุ risk factors สำหรับ BPD-associated PH และ echo signs ที่ใช้ screen

ตอบ: Patient risk factors: extreme prematurity, ELBW, SGA, oligohydramnios, early PH at day 7. Management risk factors: prolonged mechanical ventilation, PDA ligation, high O2 exposure. Echo screening at 36 wk PMA: (1) TR jet velocity → estimated RVSP (most reliable; absent in 25% — don't miss). (2) Interventricular septal flattening (qualitative). (3) RV dilation + hypertrophy. (4) ↓ Pulmonary venous velocity = ↑ PVR. Outcome: BPD-PH ↑ mortality + ↓ neurodevelopment — early detection critical for pulmonary vasodilator therapy (sildenafil, bosentan).
Q: อธิบาย physiologically based cord clamping (PBCC) — กลไกที่ดีกว่า immediate cord clamping?

ตอบ: PBCC concept: delay cord clamping until lung aeration confirmed and PBF established. Mechanism: immediate cord clamping before lung aeration → sudden loss of placental return → ↓ LA preload (main source in utero was placental return via FO) → ↓ LV preload → ↓ cardiac output → hemodynamic instability. PBCC: lung aeration first → ↑ PBF → LA filled from pulmonary veins before cord clamped → smooth transition. Bhatt 2013: PBCC vs immediate clamping in preterm lambs — much less hemodynamic instability. Practical: DCC ≥30-60 sec, or PBCC with PPV while cord intact when infant needs resuscitation.
Q: PHELBI trial (Thome 2015): high permissive hypercapnia target ใน ELBW ผลเป็นอย่างไร?

ตอบ: N=359 ELBW <28 wk 6d: high pCO2 target (55-65 mmHg) vs mild (40-50 mmHg). Primary: No difference in BPD or death. PHELBI follow-up (Arch Dis Child FN 2017): No difference in neurodevelopmental outcomes. Implication: aggressive permissive hypercapnia provides no additional benefit over moderate targets; แต่ high target เสี่ยง IVH มากขึ้น (CO2 >52 = IVH breakpoint in first days). Conclusion: target 45-55 mmHg (moderate permissive hypercapnia) — lowest risk with adequate lung protection benefit.
NL Day 18 Preview

The Newborn Lung Ch.18 — Respiratory Care in the Delivery Room
พรุ่งนี้: Respiratory management at birth — the first critical minutes — เชื่อมโยงโดยตรงกับวันนี้ (PBCC, cord clamping timing) และ Ch.16 (patient-triggered vs controlled breaths). หัวข้อหลัก: initial positive pressure ventilation (T-piece resuscitator vs bag-mask), CPAP at birth, sustained inflation (SI) evidence, oxygen targeting at birth (SpO2 reference ranges NRP 2021), laryngoscopy + intubation technique, delivery room surfactant (INSURE/LISA/MIST), resuscitation of extremely preterm infants at the threshold of viability, and physiologic transition support. เชื่อมกับวันนี้: understanding cardiorespiratory interaction → delivery room actions that ↑ hemodynamic stability, particularly lung aeration before cord clamping + avoiding hyperoxia (↓ iNO response in PPHN) + gentle initial tidal volumes (↓ volutrauma in first breaths).
